data.title: fix: preserve clean staging and kill timed-out hook trees

data.body: LOW (defense-in-depth): kill_hook_tree resolves the `kill` binary via PATH. On timeout, a PATH-poisoning attacker (or a stray `kill` shim earlier in PATH) would get code execution in the maw process context. Severity is low — this is a local dev tool that already PATH-resolves `git`/`sh`, so an attacker controlling PATH has broader reach — but a direct syscall (`libc::kill(-pid, SIGKILL)` / nix) would both close this and be more reliable than depending on a `kill` binary/builtin. Ordering is otherwise correct: kill runs before child.wait(), so the unreaped child keeps its pgid reserved and there's no PID-reuse race on the negative-PID group target.

INFO: Security-positive. Moving clean-capture to an alternate GIT_INDEX_FILE (read-tree HEAD → git add -f → git stash create) removes a destructive real-index reset that could silently discard the caller's already-staged work — a data-integrity / Prime-Invariant gap. Verified safe: `git add -f -- <paths>` uses `--` (no option injection), git rejects out-of-worktree paths (no traversal), temp index lives in a 0700 tempdir, temp_dir outlives the stash-create read, and the empty-stash guard ("clean aborted to avoid data loss") preserves the recovery guarantee.

data.reason: Security fixes verified — no CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM. capture.rs alternate-index change is a data-integrity improvement (removes destructive real-index reset); post_sync_hook.rs process-group kill is correctly ordered (no PID-reuse race). One LOW defense-in-depth note left re PATH-resolved kill.
